project_overview.name: EOMail
project_overview.type: Full-stack email application with AI features

stack.frontend: React with TypeScript, TanStack React Query, Wouter (routing), Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui components

users.notes: Password stored as bcrypt hash. Has uniqueness constraints on username and email.

emails.notes: Foreign key to users with ON DELETE CASCADE. Indexes on userId, folder, timestamp.

agent_activity.notes: Tracks autonomous AI agent actions on emails.

session.notes: Used by connect-pg-simple for session storage.

features: Login with account lockout (added in audit)

vulnerabilities_fixed.title: XSS via dangerouslySetInnerHTML in email-list.tsx
vulnerabilities_fixed.description: highlightText() rendered email sender names, subjects, and previews using dangerouslySetInnerHTML without DOMPurify sanitization. While escapeHtml() was applied, it did not provide defense-in-depth against edge cases.
vulnerabilities_fixed.fix: Added DOMPurify.sanitize() wrapping around highlightText() output.

vulnerabilities_fixed.title: Weak password policy (minimum 6 characters)
vulnerabilities_fixed.description: Minimum password length was only 6 characters, too weak for production.
vulnerabilities_fixed.fix: Increased minimum password length to 12 characters in both server and client validation.

vulnerabilities_fixed.title: Timing-unsafe webhook secret comparison
vulnerabilities_fixed.description: Webhook secret was compared using !== (string equality), vulnerable to timing attacks.
vulnerabilities_fixed.fix: Replaced with crypto.timingSafeEqual() for constant-time comparison.

vulnerabilities_fixed.title: No account lockout on failed logins
vulnerabilities_fixed.description: No mechanism to prevent brute-force login attacks beyond rate limiting.
vulnerabilities_fixed.fix: Added in-memory login attempt tracking. Accounts lock after 5 failed attempts within a 15-minute window.

vulnerabilities_fixed.title: User enumeration via registration error messages
vulnerabilities_fixed.description: Registration returned 'Username already taken', allowing attackers to enumerate valid usernames.
vulnerabilities_fixed.fix: Changed to generic message: 'An account with this username or email already exists'.

vulnerabilities_fixed.title: Missing CSP directives and security headers
vulnerabilities_fixed.description: CSP was missing frame-ancestors, form-action, base-uri, and object-src directives. No Referrer-Policy header.
vulnerabilities_fixed.fix: Added frameAncestors: ['none'], formAction: ['self'], baseUri: ['self'], objectSrc: ['none'], and referrerPolicy: 'strict-origin-when-cross-origin'.

remaining_recommendations.title: unsafe-inline in script CSP
remaining_recommendations.description: scriptSrc includes 'unsafe-inline' which weakens CSP. Should use nonce-based approach.

remaining_recommendations.title: In-memory rate limiter store
remaining_recommendations.description: Rate limiter resets on server restart. Should use Redis-backed store.

remaining_recommendations.title: Session cookie sameSite is lax
remaining_recommendations.description: Consider upgrading to 'strict' for stronger CSRF protection.

remaining_recommendations.title: No CSRF token validation
remaining_recommendations.description: Application relies on sameSite cookie for CSRF protection. Explicit CSRF tokens would add defense-in-depth.

remaining_recommendations.title: WebSocket CSP too permissive
remaining_recommendations.description: connectSrc allows ws: and wss: to any host. Should restrict to specific WebSocket host.

remaining_recommendations.title: Webhook accepts secret via query parameter
remaining_recommendations.description: req.query.secret fallback exposes the secret in URL and server logs. Only accept via header.

remaining_recommendations.title: No password complexity requirements
remaining_recommendations.description: Passwords only enforce minimum length, not character diversity.

remaining_recommendations.title: Long session duration (30 days)
remaining_recommendations.description: Long-lived sessions increase window for session hijacking.

remaining_recommendations.title: API response logging includes full JSON bodies
remaining_recommendations.description: Logged response bodies at server/index.ts:72 could contain sensitive user data.

secure_areas: SQL injection prevention via Drizzle ORM parameterized queries
Input validation with Zod schemas using strict mode
PII redaction before sending data to OpenAI (credit cards, SSNs, API keys, passwords, bank accounts)
No eval(), exec(), or dynamic code execution anywhere
Password reset tokens use crypto.randomBytes(32) with 1-hour expiry
No hardcoded credentials or secrets in codebase
No sensitive data in localStorage (only AI command history)
Static routes only (no dynamic redirects based on user input)

ai_features: AI spam detection with scoring and reasons
AI chat panel for email interaction
AI command bar with localStorage history
other_observations.client_auth_flow: ProtectedRoute component checks useAuth() hook, redirects to /auth if not authenticated. Server-side validation is the primary security boundary.
other_observations.xcode_request: User asked if I could access Xcode on their MacBook. Answer: No - I only have access to the Linux environment where EOMail is hosted.
